title: Source: Biden team finds more docs with classified markings
shortDescription: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Joe Biden’s legal team has discovered additional documents containing classification markings in a second location, a person familiar with the matter told The Associated Press on Wednesday.

title: White House: Jill Biden has two cancerous lesions removed
shortDescription: WASHINGTON (AP) — Surgeons removed a cancerous lesion above first lady Jill Biden 's right eye and one on her chest, the White House said Wednesday, while a third lesion on her left eyelid was being examined.

title: Republicans push anti-abortion measures with new majority
shortDescription: WASHINGTON (AP) — House Republicans are taking early action on abortion with their new majority, approving two measures Wednesday that make clear they want further restraints after the Supreme Court overruled the federal right to abortion last year.

title: George Santos refuses to resign despite political pressure
shortDescription: NEW YORK (AP) — State and local Republican leaders in New York on Wednesday called for the immediate resignation of their new GOP congressman George Santos, who is facing multiple investigations by prosecutors over his personal and campaign finances and lies about his resume and family heritage.

title: GOP opens long-promised investigation into Biden family
shortDescription: WASHINGTON (AP) — House Republicans on Wednesday opened their long-promised investigation into President Joe Biden and his family, wielding the power of their new majority to demand information from the Treasury Department and former Twitter executives as they laid the groundwork for public hearings.

title: Supreme Court lets New York enforce gun law during lawsuit
shortDescription: WASHINGTON (AP) — New York can for now continue to enforce a sweeping new law that bans guns from “sensitive places” including schools, playgrounds and Times Square, the Supreme Court said Wednesday, allowing the law to be in force while a lawsuit over it plays out.

title: Unity is new tone for often-divided Democrats in Washington
shortDescription: WASHINGTON (AP) — The infighting was so intense a year ago that Democrats who controlled both the White House and Congress couldn't win support for a sweeping social spending package that was the party's top legislative priority.

title: US border authorities roll out updated pursuit policy
shortDescription: ALBUQUERQUE, N.M. (AP) — U.S. border authorities announced changes to their policy for pursuing smugglers and other crime suspects on Wednesday, following an extensive review and criticism by immigrant advocates who pointed to cases in which passengers died when drivers fled law enforcement.

title: Larger GOP majorities seek impact at N. Carolina Statehouse
shortDescription: RALEIGH, N.C. (AP) — The North Carolina General Assembly opened its two-year legislative session Wednesday with Republicans on the cusp of veto-proof control that will force Democratic Gov.

title: Maine governor’s 2-year budget tops $10 billion
shortDescription: AUGUSTA, Maine (AP) — Democratic Gov. Janet Mills on Wednesday unveiled a $10.3 billion, two-year budget proposal that builds on her previous priorities without raising taxes or raiding the state’s reserves.

title: LA Council again struggles with fallout from racism scandal
shortDescription: LOS ANGELES (AP) — The Los Angeles City Council struggled again Wednesday with a still-unfolding racism scandal that led to the resignation of its former president and left behind a quandary about how to deal with a disgraced member who has resisted calls from President Joe Biden to step down.

title: With budget surplus, Alabama lawmakers consider tax rebates
shortDescription: MONTGOMERY, Ala. (AP) — Alabama lawmakers are eyeing possible tax rebates as the state sees a budget surplus of more than $2.7 billion.Legislators will face decisions on what to do with the rare surplus, including how much to save and how to spend the rest.

title: Amid Mississippi water woes, proposal could unseat mayors
shortDescription: JACKSON, Miss. (AP) — As Mississippi's capital city of Jackson struggles with water problems and its Democratic mayor clashes with the Republican governor, lawmakers debated a proposal Wednesday that would allow recall elections for municipal officials in the state.

title: Minnesota joins other states in affordable housing promises
shortDescription: ST. PAUL, Minn. (AP) — Weeks after the Biden administration announced new efforts to curb homelessness, Minnesota's top political leaders promised Wednesday to “bring it home” on legislation that would expand affordable housing.

title: AG wants Kansas court to rethink abortion rights protections
shortDescription: TOPEKA, Kan. (AP) — Kansas' anti-abortion Republican attorney general is asking the state's highest court to reconsider a landmark decision protecting access to abortion months after a decisive statewide vote affirming abortion rights.

title: Arkoosh to lead Shapiro’s sprawling human services agency
shortDescription: HARRISBURG, Pa. (AP) — Dr. Val Arkoosh, an anesthesiologist who led Pennsylvania’s third-most populous county through the pandemic before mounting a failed run for U.S. Senate, will be nominated to lead the sprawling Department of Human Services under the incoming Democratic governor, Josh Shapiro.

title: Wisconsin Elections Commissioner stands by voting comments
shortDescription: MADISON, Wis. (AP) — A Republican member of Wisconsin's bipartisan elections commission on Wednesday stood by comments he made crowing about depressed turnout among Black and Hispanic voters in heavily Democratic Milwaukee, saying he won't resign as a fellow commissioner and others have called on him to do.

title: Georgia lawmakers push back on testifying in Trump probe
shortDescription: ATLANTA (AP) — Georgia state lawmakers are pushing back on a state court ruling that forced some of them to testify during an investigation into whether former President Donald Trump and others illegally tried to influence the 2020 election in the state.

title: Appeals court hears arguments on Voting Rights Act scope
shortDescription: LITTLE ROCK, Ark. (AP) — A federal appeals court heard arguments Wednesday over whether the Voting Rights Act allows private citizens to sue to enforce a key part of the 1965 law prohibiting discriminatory voting practices.

title: Taxes, abortion on agenda as Virginia lawmakers back at work
shortDescription: RICHMOND, Va. (AP) — The Virginia General Assembly kicked off its annual legislative session Wednesday with a hefty agenda that includes taxes, abortion and energy policy, but there are low expectations for how much work will actually get done during an election year.

title: Ohio lawmaker not speaker but says he still leads House GOP
shortDescription: COLUMBUS, Ohio (AP) — A conservative state lawmaker who lost to a fellow Republican in last week's vote to determine who will lead the Ohio House as speaker said Wednesday that he has the support of most of the members of his party in the chamber.

title: Minnesota lawmakers start down path to legalizing marijuana
shortDescription: ST. PAUL, Minn. (AP) — The Minnesota Legislature embarked on a path toward legalizing recreational marijuana for adults Wednesday when a legislative committee held the first hearing of the year on a bill that backers say is designed to avoid the pitfalls experienced by states that have already legalized it.

title: W.Va. GOP-controlled Senate fast-tracks race, health bills
shortDescription: CHARLESTON, W.Va. (AP) — The Republican-dominated Senate in West Virginia fast-tracked two monumental health proposals and another bill that would restrict how subjects like race are taught in the classroom during the first day of its legislative session Wednesday.

title: Republican concedes in Virginia state Senate election
shortDescription: VIRGINIA BEACH, Va. (AP) — The Democrat in a closely watched eastern Virginia state Senate race appeared headed to victory after his opponent conceded on Wednesday in the special election. The outcome appears to boost the Democrats’ narrow control of the state Senate, with abortion among the issues expected to be debated during the legislative session that also starts Wednesday.

title: Pennsylvania Senate bill aims to undo 2023 gas tax increase
shortDescription: HARRISBURG, Pa. (AP) — Pennsylvania's state Senate on Wednesday voted to undo an automatic wholesale tax increase on gasoline and diesel that kicks in this year in an effort to prevent motorists from paying more per gallon.

title: New push to restrict abortions to be introduced in Nebraska
shortDescription: LINCOLN, Neb. (AP) — A Nebraska lawmaker who sponsored a failed total abortion ban last year announced Wednesday that she will introduce a new bill this week that would ban abortion once cardiac activity can be detected in an embryo, which is generally around the sixth week of pregnancy.

title: Constitutional amendments pose test to incoming Pa. governor
shortDescription: HARRISBURG, Pa. (AP) — Republicans who control Pennsylvania's Senate on Wednesday kicked off the new legislative session by pushing through a trio of proposed constitutional amendments that sparked a partisan fight and poses a challenge to the incoming Democratic governor, Josh Shapiro.

title: Ex-Louisiana lawmaker gets 22-month sentence for wire fraud
shortDescription: NEW ORLEANS (AP) — Former Louisiana Democratic Party leader Karen Carter Peterson, who resigned from the state Senate last year year citing depression and a gambling addiction — and later pleaded guilty to federal wire fraud — was sentenced on Wednesday to 22 months in prison.

title: Tennessee House eases public view of proposed amendments
shortDescription: NASHVILLE, Tenn. (AP) — The Tennessee House is offering the public use of an online platform that makes it easier to read proposed amendments to bills.Republican House Speaker Cameron Sexton announced Wednesday that the TGA House Dashboard application offers the public the same real-time view of proposed and approved amendments that lawmakers have.

title: Questions remain about Biden classified docs as DOJ review nears end
shortDescription: The review move towards an inflection point with many questions unanswered. President Joe Biden has maintained the matter was handled appropriately.

title: Katie Porter is running for senate. Get to know the oversite maven
shortDescription: Congresswoman Katie Porter has announced her run for U.S. Senate. Here's what you need to know about the California representative.

title: Second batch of Biden documents located, reports say
shortDescription: The disclosure comes a day after Biden told reporters he was "surprised" by the discovery of the first batch of documents.

title: Republicans call for on George Santos to resign
shortDescription: New York Republicans are demanding Rep. George Santos resign after he admitted to lying about his education, work experience and being Jewish.

title: White House won't say when Biden learned about classified documents
shortDescription: The White House pointed to an ongoing Justice Department review, declining to answer when President Joe Biden knew about the classified documents.

title: Lawmakers vow probe of FAA outage; GOP blames Biden administration
shortDescription: Congress plans to investigate the FAA"s computer outage that cancelled more than 7,300 flights and sparked outrage from Republican lawmakers.

title: NY Republicans urge Santos to resign, McCarthy says it's up to the voters
shortDescription: Speaker Kevin McCarthy and Majority Leader Steve Scalise told reporters they had not seen the call from New York Republicans for Santos to step down.

title: Biden orders FAA outage investigation
shortDescription: President Joe Biden ordered an investigation into the cause of a Federal Aviation Administration outage that caused thousands of flight delays.

title: Supreme Court keeps New York's expansive handgun law in place for now
shortDescription: New York Gov. Kathy Hochul signed the law after a Supreme Court ruling in June made it far easier for many Americans to carry handguns in public.

title: Biden says he was surprised to learn there were classified documents in his office
shortDescription: Biden addressed the classified documents found in an old office while in Mexico City, where he met with the leaders of Canada and Mexico.

title: McCarthy's 'secret deal,' Santos, classified docs: new Congress takeaways
shortDescription: Democrats and Republicans in Congress are beginning the new session in turmoil over a "secret deal," committee positions, Trump, Biden and more.

title: Shipping container barrier to cost AZ taxpayers $200M
shortDescription: Arizona taxpayers will foot the bill to put up and take down former Gov. Doug Ducey's shipping container border barrier.

title: Mexican president thanks Biden for not adding to border wall
shortDescription: Mexican President Andrés Manuel López Obrador thanked President Joe Biden for not adding to the wall along the U.S.-Mexican border.

title: Biden comments on classified documents found
shortDescription: President Joe Biden said he's cooperating with the National Archives after classified documents were found in an old office in Washington, D.C.

title: Investigating the investigators: New GOP House panels to probe DOJ, China
shortDescription: Republicans are keeping their campaign promises after winning the House majority, launching investigations into Biden and his administration.

title: OnPolitics: Biden's documents vs. Trump's
shortDescription: The White House contrasted his returning documents voluntarily with Trump holding his records.

title: Ex-Trump Organization CFO Allen Weisselberg sentenced to 5 months
shortDescription: After helping prosecutors win criminal tax fraud convictions of two Trump companies, the ex-Trump Organization CFO gets lenient sentence under deal.

title: What we know about President Joe Biden's classified documents
shortDescription: Classified records found in an office once used by Joe Biden echoes the discovery at Donald Trump's Mar-a-Lago. What we know about the two cases.

title: GOP leaders seeking Biden investigation
shortDescription: Democrats and Republicans have two different opinions on how to proceed after classified records were found in an office once used by Joe Biden.

title: Rep. Katie Porter announces California Senate bid
shortDescription: The announcement comes despite no official indication longtime Democratic Sen. Dianne Feinstein, whose up for reelection in 2024, is retiring.

title: How the GOP got here: Rise of hardliners from Goldwater to MAGA
shortDescription: Goldwater, Reagan, the Tea Party all made their marks but ultra-conservatives last week might have made the biggest gains in the House speaker fight.

title: DOJ reviewing classified documents at Biden facility from time as VP
shortDescription: Richard Sauber, a special counsel to the president, said the documents were immediately turned over the National Archives.

title: Georgia grand jury completes Trump probe
shortDescription: Fulton County Superior Court Judge Robert McBurney set a Jan. 24 hearing to determine whether the report would be made public.

title: Biden seeks Mexico's AMLO help in stopping deadly fentanyl
shortDescription: Joe Biden kicks off the North American Leaders' Summit asking Mexican President Andrés Manuel López Obrador for help tackling the fentanyl crisis.

title: U.S. House rules approved for 118th Congress
shortDescription: U.S. House rules approved for the 118th Congress with concessions that range from reducing government spending to freedom caucus members getting more seats.

title: Six Albuquerque Democrats' homes, offices targeted by shootings: police
shortDescription: A suspect is in custody in connection with an apparent string of shootings targeting Democratic lawmakers homes and offices in Albuquerque, N.M.

title: In Child Welfare Cases, Most of Your Constitutional Rights Don’t Apply
shortDescription: The child welfare system rarely offers the same rights as the criminal justice system, leaving many families facing permanent separation without due process protections.

title: House Passes Bill That Could Subject Some Abortion Doctors to Prosecution
shortDescription: As part of an anti-abortion rights effort, Republicans pushed through a bill laying out criminal penalties for doctors who fail to resuscitate babies born after an attempted abortion.By Annie Karni

title: The Special Counsel Investigating Trump Faces Headwinds Few Others Have
shortDescription: Jack Smith’s challenges include a newly empowered Republican House majority and the recent disclosures of President Biden’s retention of classified documents.By Katie Benner, Adam Goldman, Glenn Thrush and Maggie Haberman

title: George Santos Faces Calls to Resign From 4 G.O.P. Congressmen
shortDescription: Republican officials in New York sharply denounced the embattled representative, even as leaders in Washington have said they will not push him to step down.By Michael Gold and Grace Ashford

title: Katie Porter, California Congresswoman, Is Running for Senate
shortDescription: Ms. Porter is the first announced challenger to Senator Dianne Feinstein, 89, who has yet to declare her 2024 plans but is widely expected to not seek re-election.By Reid J. Epstein

title: Cherokee tribes turn to K Street to fulfill a 187-year-old promise for a seat in Congress
shortDescription: But they aren’t in agreement over who should get the largely ceremonial post.
